observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:

Prepare and maintain subsidiary ledgers for the financial accounts of the LGU. These ledgers shall be the source of information to prepare schedules to facilitate verification of the accounts in the financial statements; and
Equip herself of the technical knowledge and skills on the preparation of the ledgers by reading prevailing manuals and issuances on the matter and asking guidance from the Auditors.

We recommended that Management strictly adhere to the policies and procedures prescribed for effective control and accounting of supplies and materials and:

The Municipal Accountant stop the practice of using expense method I recording supplies and materials purchases, maintain an inventory account using perpetual inventory system and prepare supplies ledger card per inventory item;
The Municipal Treasurer maintain supplies and materials stock card per stock number and prepare Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued and submit the same to the Municipal Accountant for updating of its inventory records; and 
The Inventory Committee conduct physical count of inventories at least once a year to facilitate establishment of inventory ending balance that will be reflected in the financial statements.
observations.reason: Ongoing preparation of the internal control system

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer prepare and issue PAR or ICS to the end-users or recipients for all properties owned by the LGU to establish accountability and ensure judicious use thereof pursuant to COA Circular No. 92-386 and Government Accounting Manual for LGUs.
observations.reason: Ongoing preparation in setting up control system.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management strengthen the installation of an effective inventory management system to make sound analysis on the receipt, distribution and/or utilization of in-kind donations by adhering COA Circular No. 2014-002 as amended by COA Circular No. 2020-009. Require the concerned offices for the receipt and distribution to submit immediately a Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances and the Accounting Office and the Property Unit to record timely the in-kind donations in the books or in the registries.
observations.reason: Ongoing preparation in setting up control system.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended the following measure in order to correct the above noted deficiencies:

The management ensure that all disbursement vouchers with attached supporting documents are forwarded to the Budget Office for obligation and monitoring prior to payment thereof; and 
The Municipal Budget Officer ensure that certification as to existence of available appropriation be done in all financial transactions and prepare and maintain the required Registries of Appropriation, Allotments and Obligations (RAAO) for Capital Outlays (CO), Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses (MOOE), Personal Services (PS) and Financial Expenses (FE) per responsibility center.
observations.reason: Ongoing preparation in setting up control system.

observations.recommendation: We recommend and the Management agreed to create an Inventory Committee that will spearhead in the conduct of annual physical count of all properties owned by the Municipality and prepare report thereon to be submitted to COA not later than January 31 of each year. Moreover, the Treasury Office and the Accounting Office agreed to prepare and maintain Property Cards and PPE Ledgers Cards, respectively and ensure that reconciliation of their records is done before preparations of the annual financial statements.
observations.reason: No inventory committee assigned yet. Ongoing preparation in setting up control system.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Management to designate an official property custodian or a supply officer who shall be tasked to maintain and update Property Cards per category of property, plant and equipment, including the unserviceable properties, install control to keep safe the PPEs of the municipality like the preparation and maintenance of Requisition and Issue Slips, the practice of property tagging for property identification and monitoring, maintenance of ARE, etc.; create an inventory committee/team that will spearhead in planning and conducting physical count of the Municipality’s properties and to prepare and submit to the Audit Team the Report on Physical Count of PPE pursuant to Section 124 of NGAS Manual for LGU; instruct the Municipal Accountant to maintain and update the Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards for each category of assets; and adhere to the provisions on property management set forth in the above-stated regulations and other pertinent laws, rules and regulations.
observations.reason: Ongoing preparation in setting up control system.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant shall ensure that all procured equipment and supplies shall be properly recognized and recorded to its appropriate PPE and inventory accounts. In addition, direct the Accounting and the Supply Units to prepare and maintain PPE Ledger and property Cards for all PPE and as well as issue ARE to the accountable personnel and That Municipal Accountant shall affect the proposed adjusting journal entry in the books.

Formulate a comprehensive and integrated LDRRMP.  Appropriate at least 5% of the total estimated revenue for LDRRMF to have ample fund to defray expenses for the reduction of disaster risks and enhancement of disaster preparedness and response capabilities of the municipality; transfer from general fund ample, if not all, cash allocation to the LDRRMF to fund all its identified programs and activities.  Accrue and transfer the unexpended portion of the LDRRMF at the end of the year to a special trust fund in compliance with Section 21 of R.A. No. 10121; plan, develop and implement only PPAs that are implementable in a given period.

Submit to the DSWD, in a manner prescribed in the MOA, the updated list of eligible senior citizens, monthly accomplishment report of the program, SORD, List of paid and unpaid beneficiaries certified by the municipal accountant and signed by the municipal mayor and the checks for refund of the unexpended balance; and adhere strictly to the provisions in the MOA on the receipt and utilization of funds intended for the social pension program.

Maintain the required registries of funds/appropriations/allotments and obligations/utilization and shall certify on their availability, in order to avoid juggling of funds; for internal control purposes; and for the realization of the programs/projects/activities embodied in the approved annual budget.
observations.reason: Ongoing preparation in setting up control system.

observations.recommendation: Observe and comply to the provisions as herein provided, otherwise there would be incurrence of irregular transactions which will be disallowed in audit, being procured not in accordance with the established laws, rules and regulations, and in order that reasonableness of the amount spent will also be determined, so that possible loss of government funds will be avoided.
observations.reason: Ongoing preparation in setting up control system.

observations.recommendation: Instruct the Property and Supply Section to submit the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption to the Auditor within ten (10) days of the succeeding month.
observations.reason: No appointed property and supply custodian. Ongoing preparation in setting up control system.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to conduct regular monthly and quarterly reconciliation of their books of accounts and prepare report of reconciliation. Likewise, submit a reconciliation statement of accountability for us to make a decision in audit.

We recommended that management direct the Municipal Accountant to facilitate monthly preparation and submission of Bank Reconciliation Statements. Likewise, submit the BRS to the Office of the Auditor within ten (10) days of the ensuing month and thereafter.

We recommended that Management direct Inventory Committee to conduct of annual physical count of all properties owned by the Municipality and prepare a report thereon to be submitted to COA not later than January 31 of each year. Moreover, the Treasury Office and the Accounting Office to prepare and maintain Property Cards and PPE Ledgers Cards, respectively and ensure that reconciliation of their records is done before preparation of the annual financial statements.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant with the certified list of taxpayers with their corresponding tax due. In turn, the latter recognize RPT/SET Receivable at the beginning of the year and properly record the initial and subsequent recognitions.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Treasurer to transfer and deposit the remaining ₱3,927,402.04 from General Fund to the 20% DF account immediately so that funds can be used for its intended purposes. Moving forward, ensure that the total amounts transferred to 20% DF represent the statutory 20% of the Internal Revenue Allotment (IRA).

We recommended that Management direct allocate funds for Disaster Recovery and Rehabilitation area as this is also important in disaster and risk management. Further, require the Municipal Budget Officer to prepare separate RAAO for LDRRMF so that proper charging to the fund can be easily identifiable and traceable. With regard to disbursement, refrain from using the fund to purposes that do not partake to any of the disaster and risk management thematic area.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to transfer the unutilized balance of LDRRMF for CY 2021 and prior years, if applicable, from the General Fund to a Special Trust Fund account. Likewise, ensure that the Municipal Accountant observe the accounting for the unexpended LDRRMF balance and the required disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements.

We recommended that Management direct the Human Resource and Management Officer to review the Job Order contracts and effect changes as necessary, especially the rate to be used in wage computation and enforce “no work, no pay” principle. Further, ensure that DTRs are correctly prepared and duly signed by the immediate supervisor before preparing corresponding payrolls. Likewise, the Municipal Accountant ensure that proper rate and number of days were used in the computation of wages due the JO workers and do not process for payment without complete supporting documents.

We recommended that Management strictly adhere to the provisions provided in the Local Government Code of 1991 in the allocation of EME. Further, require the Municipal Budget Officer to carefully compute the statutory limitation on funds for discretionary purposes and monitor the charges against the fund to avoid excessive expenditures. Likewise, require the Municipal Accountant to properly classify transactions of the Extraordinary and Miscellaneous Expenses account.

We recommended that Management direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to ensure that procurement of Common-Use Supplies and Equipment (CSE) are procured in the DBM-PS. If unavailable, secure a Certificate of Non-availability of Stocks (CNAS) or its equivalent from DBM-PS and attach the same to the purchase request/purchase order before procuring from other sources.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain Journals and Ledgers in the prescribed forms that can be found in Volume II of the New Government Accounting System Manual. Likewise, maintain separate books of accounts and financial reports for Special Accounts in the General Fund and Trust Fund.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Budget Officer to keep and maintain the required registries, namely, RAAOPS, RAAOME, RAAOCO, RAAOFE, in a manner and form prescribed in the New Government Accounting System Manual.

We recommended and the Municipal Treasurer or the Disbursing Officer agreed to, if any, stamp “PAID” and date all paid disbursement vouchers and it’s supporting documents to preclude double payment of the same transactions.

We recommended that Management direct the BAC Secretariat to ensure that the requisite information is contained in purchase order and submit the same and all related supporting documents within five (5) days after its perfection for evaluation and review.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Treasurer to continue exerting extra effort and adopt the remedies to enforce
collection of delinquent real property taxes in accordance with RA No. 7160.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
verify and confirm with the Provincial Accountant the discrepancies of
P742,037.33 and P412,298.97 between the recorded balances of the reciprocal
accounts reflected in the books of the Province and adjust the same to present the correct balances of the said accounts in the Municipality’s financial statement.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the General Services Officer
(GSO) to dispose the unserviceable properties in accordance with Section 79 of
PD No. 1445 to avoid further deterioration and earn at least the salvage value from
the sale of these assets. Likewise, create an Appraisal and Disposal Committee
who will be responsible in appraising the scrap/salvage value of those unserviceable properties.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant request for snap shots from the bank every end of the month. These documents could be used in lieu of the bank statements to facilitate timely preparation and submission of Bank Reconciliation Statements to support Management’s assertion on the existence and accuracy of the reported valuation of Cash in Bank-LCCA in the financial statements.
observations.reason: Difficulty in reconciling due to lack of supporting bank statements.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management create an Inventory Committee that will spearhead in the conduct of annual physical count of all properties owned by the Municipality and prepare a report thereon to be submitted to COA not later than January 31 of each year. Moreover, the Treasury Office and the Accounting Office agreed to prepare and maintain Property Cards and PPE Ledgers Cards, respectively and ensure that reconciliation of their records is done before preparation of the annual financial statements.
observations.reason: No inventory committee assigned yet. Awaiting management’s action.

Ensure that financial transactions are properly recognized and recorded in the bools in accordance with Section 112 of PD No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2015-009.

We recommended that the Municipality refrain from paying additional remuneration to JO workers other than what is entitled to them and support disbursements with complete documentary requirements in adherence to the fundamental principles set forth in Section 4 of PD 1445 and likewise observe pertinent Civil Service rules and regulations. In addition, we require the Municipal Mayor, Municipal Accountant-Designate, Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Budget Officer to submit immediately to the Audit Team written justification in relation to these transactions.

We recommended that the Management fill out the vacant position of Municipal Accountant, as prescribed in Section 443 of RA 7160 and in accordance with existing guidelines and rules and regulations of the Civil Service Commission for an effective administration of the Accounting Office which in turn will benefit of the Municipality as a whole.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and Officials concerned ensure that at the start of every budget period, the end-user units of the Municipality shall prepare their respective PPMP in the prescribed format for their different programs, activities and projects (PAPs) and corresponding Annual Procurement Plan (APP) in accordance with Sections 7.1 to 7.4 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184.
observations.reason: Lack of personnel and BAC TWG for the implementation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC submit to the auditor concerned required documents enumerated in COA Circular 2009-001 for the projects implemented in CY 2020 for auditorial review. Further, all future projects contracts of the Municipality, together with the documentary requirements, shall be submitted to the auditor concerned for review.

We recommended to Management that expenses inappropriately charged to the 20% DF be transferred and charged against General Fund Proper so that the amounts can be returned for appropriate development projects, otherwise, the amount will be disallowed in audit, being irregular expenditure. Likewise, the Local Chief Executive ensure that the 20% DF of the Municipality be utilized only to fund priority programs or projects included in the approved AIP which partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures in compliance with the provisions of DILG-DBM JMC. No 2017-1.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive issue an Executive Order or Administrative Order to create a Monitoring and Evaluation Team and the GAD Focal Point System prepare the necessary status and evaluation reports for submission to DILG.

We recommended and the Management agreed to the following measures in order to correct the above noted deficiencies:

Ensure that all disbursement vouchers with attached supporting documents are forwarded to the Budget Office for obligation and monitoring prior to payment thereof;
The Municipal Budget Officer ensures that certification as to existence of available appropriation be done in all financial transactions; and
The Municipal Budget Officer prepares and maintains the required Registries of Appropriation, Allotments and Obligations (RAAO) for Capital Outlays (CO), Maintenance and Other Expenses (MOOE), Personal Services (PS) and Financial Expenses (FE) per responsibility center.
observations.reason: No registries maintained. Ongoing preparation in setting up control system.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the Management agreed to administer the HGDG in the GAD Plan and Budget, and GAD Accomplishment Report.
observations.reason: Lack of technical workforce to assist the MSWDO.

observations.recommendation: We recommend and the Management agreed to refrain from charging financial assistance to individuals from the GAD Budget without conducting appropriate gender analysis and/or gender assessment and ensure that the planned GAD programs and activities are truly gender responsive.
observations.reason: Expenses charged were approved by GAD focal person.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the Municipal Accountant to ensure that the required VAT, EVAT and Percentage taxes on all government payments to its suppliers are properly deducted, withheld and be remitted within the prescribed period in accordance with Section 12 of RA No. 9337 and Section 4.112 of Revenue Regulation (RR) No. 16-2005.

We recommended that Management regularly submit the list of all on-going government PPAs and those that are to be implemented during the year and ensure that the information presented therein is complete in accordance with above cited provisions.

We recommended for the Management to instruct the Accounting Office to strictly submit the accounts, trial balances and financial statements within the prescribed period required under Section 41 of Presidential Decree No. 1445 (PD 1445), Section 347 of Local Government Code of 1991 and COA Circular No. 2009-006 and remind the concerned official or employee that failure to comply shall cause the automatic suspension of the payment of his salary and other emoluments until he shall have complied therewith and violation of these provisions for at least three times shall subject the offender to administrative disciplinary action.
observations.reason: Difficulty in retrieving supporting documents from different offices. Lack of personnel to perform accounting processes.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the Management to instruct the Cashier or the Municipal Treasurer that henceforth, all paid disbursement vouchers and its supporting documents be properly stamped “PAID” and dated as to payment to preclude double payment of the same transactions.

We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the accountant to draw Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to record all the valid reconciling items that require adjustment and correction on the General Ledger and require the Municipal Treasurer to adjust her cashbook effecting the reconciling items and have it reconciled with the accounting records for the fair presentation of the Cash in Bank account in the financial statements.

observations.reason: Accounting Office is having difficulty retrieving the unliquidated cash advances and its supporting documents due to non-compliance of requested officers/ employees especially long outstanding items.
The required documents were not submitted to the audit team

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to stop granting cash advances other than travel expenses to elective officials of the Municipality. Strictly enforce Section 339 of Republic Act (RA) No. 7160 and Item Nos. 4.1.4 and 4.1.5 of COA Circular No. 97-002 in the granting of cash advances to elective and appointive officials.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Engineer to consistently prepare program of works and detailed engineering design before prosecuting any of its infrastructure projects in the future in order to comply with Section 477 b (3) & (4) of RA 7160 and Item 3(i) of Annex A of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office (LDRRMO) to prepare and submit to the concerned offices the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) which shall present the 30 percent allocation for Quick Response Fund in lump-sum and the 70 percent allocation for disaster mitigation, prevention and preparedness with details as to priority projects and activities to be implemented in the ensuing year pursuant to Section 12( c)and 21 of RA 10121 and COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended and Management agreed to transfer the deficient amount of ₱3,007,542.05 from General Fund to LDRRMF and ensure the appropriation for LDRRMF have full cash back-up to have ample fund to defray disaster-related expenses in time of emergencies and for the implementation of PPAs.
observations.reason: Dependent on the availability of funds.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to require the Offices of the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to conduct reconciliation of the account balances per Cashbook and per General Ledger quarterly or as often as needed. Identify the items that need adjustments and record them appropriately in the affected books to ascertain accuracy of the reported cash balances.
observations.reason: Municipal Accountant will start a bank reconciliation process for bank balances starting CY2021 and will inform the Municipal Treasurer on any reconciling items needing adjustments.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to expedite the completion of the BUB projects implemented by the Municipality. Further, the Municipal Engineer shall closely supervise and monitor its projects for the attainment of the desired developments projects for the constituents of the Municipality.

We recommended and Management required the Municipal Health Officer to attach a certification that the drug products being requisitioned or procured fall within and conform with PNDF Volume I, current edition in compliance with Paragraph II.2 of DOH AO No. 163 s. 2002.

We recommended and Management agreed to conduct competitive bidding in all its procurement as required in Section 10 of the Revised IRR of R.A. 9184.  Alternative methods of procurement shall only be used when conditions allow; submit to this Office the justification on the occurrence of the irregularity.  Also, submit the DVs and its supporting documents pertaining to the 47 checks with a total amount of P1,130,314.59 and furnish the Audit Team a certification from VMLP that the donated amount was indeed received and the other lacking documents mentioned above.  Strictly adhere to the provisions of R.A. 9184 and PD 1445.
observations.reason: There are still some procurements without public bidding

observations.recommendation: We recommend that Management make certain that all barangay shares from the Real Property collections are remitted to the LGUs concerned within the prescribed period in accordance with Section 271(d) of RA 7160.

Direct the Municipal Accountant to provide depreciation on the foregoing-stated PPEs to correctly present their balances in the financial statements.  Moreover, conduct appraisal, through an appraisal committee, the value and useful life of the subject PPEs as basis for the computation of depreciation expense in case historical data of said PPEs are no longer available.

observations.recommendation: Make sure that all job orders shall be covered with job order contracts; seek SB authority/resolution allowing the Municipal Mayor to enter into contract with job orders and to incur obligation in paying the wages of the same; ensure that all payments of job orders’ wages shall always be supported with DTRs and accomplishment reports and to submit the same to the Accounting before the preparation of the payroll to make sure that payments thereof will pertain to actual no. of days worked; instruct the Municipal Accountant to make corresponding adjusting entry to correct the erroneous entry made and to record in the books all the financial transactions in accordance with the accounting rules and standards.

Stop the payment of benefits to its officials, employees and job orders without legal basis; submit to the Auditor legal basis on the receipt of the benefits and justification, if there is any, on the occurrence of the irregularity; advice the Accountant to record in the books the financial transactions in accordance with the Philippine Public Sector Accounting Standards (PPSAS).

Support all claims with complete documents; faithfully adhere to all laws and regulations applicable to financial transactions of any government agency; ensure that all financial transactions shall be recorded in the books in accordance with the prevailing accounting rules and standards.  Instruct the Accountant to make the suggested adjusting journal entries in the books.

Ensure that money under Trust Fund shall always be used for specific purpose.  Submit to the Auditor justification/explanation on the occurrence of the irregularity.  Strictly adhere to Section 94 of NGAS for LGUs, Volume 1 and Section 4(3) of P.D. No. 1445 in the proper recording and utilization of amounts under Trust Fund.

Stop the practice of charging against the 20% development fund the expenditures which do not have direct contribution to the attainment of desirable socio-economic development and do not partake the nature of investment/capital expenditures.  refrain from charging against the fund the expenditures which do not have corresponding appropriation to comply with Section 4.1 of P.D. No. 1445; provide the Auditors with documents as proof that the cash assistance and cash transfer made in the amount of P11,800.00 and P220,000.00, respectively, were all spent in accordance with the objectives set forth in the DBM and DILG JMC No. 2011-1.

Conduct competitive bidding in all its procurement as required in Section 10 of the Revised IRR of R.A. 9184.  Alternative methods of procurement shall only be used when conditions allow; submit to this Office the justification on the occurrence of the irregularity.  Also furnish the Audit Team the lacking documents mentioned above.  Failure to comply may cause the suspension or disallowance of the transactions; and strictly adhere to the provision of Section 10 of R.A. 9184.

Request from the legislative department to issue a resolution authorizing the Mayor to open a trust account for the exclusive use of the DSWD supplementing feeding program in compliance with the MOA; ensure compliance with R.A. 9184 in the purchase of materials needed in the feeding program; advise the municipal treasurer to read the provisions in the MOA to acquaint herself with the requirements to comply.  She must liquidate immediately every funds received after their implementation; consolidate and submit to the DSWD field offices, copy furnished the provincial offices, the monthly physical and financial report and quarterly report on nutritional status of children beneficiaries in compliance with the requirement in the MOA.

Ensure that cash advances shall be drawn for specific purpose.  In no case it shall be used for other purposes other than the one it was granted; make sure that cash advances for salaries and wages shall at all times equal to the amount reflected in the payroll sheets. These cash advances shall always be supported with DVs and payroll sheets; refund/return to the Treasury any unexpended or unutilized amount of the cash advance; and strictly adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 on the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.

Refrain from utilizing funds without corresponding appropriation. Ensure that all payments were in line with the appropriation approved for such purpose.  Submit to the Audit Team justification on the occurrence of the irregularity.  Failure to do so may cause the suspension or disallowance of the transactions; and strictly adhere to Section 4.1 of PD 1445.

Delegate some of the MPDC’s works and obligations to other competent municipal employee so that the former may find more time to function as a municipal engineer.  Hasten the implementation of the project so that people could immediately benefit therefrom; ensure that every program, project or activity received from any sources should be immediately commenced in accordance with the guidelines and standards governing thereof so that benefit therefrom will be promptly availed; and make sure that every project, program or activity of the LGU should be kept/maintained with complete records or documents like the project proposal, program of work, detailed engineering design, bidding documents, accomplishment reports, billings, paid vouchers and the like.
Implement the programs, projects and activities as scheduled in order that benefits will be availed by the beneficiaries on time. Plan and develop only PPAs that are implementable in a given period.

continue exerting extra effort and adopt the remedies to enforce collection of

Treasurer to:

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor:
issue an office order designating a responsible person that will locate the copy of Deed of Donation for the donated cemetery lot and will facilitate the transfer of title of these five lots in the name of the Municipality to avoid contingent claims that may arise in the future and in compliance with Section 148 of COA Circular No. 92-386;
require the Municipal Treasurer (MT) to exert all efforts to collect the real property taxes from the former owners of the four lots so as to transfer their Tax Declaration in the name of the Municipality; and
require the Municipal Budget Officer (MBO) to provide appropriate funding for the said undertaking.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the MT to exert extra effort and adopt remedies to enforce collection of delinquent real property taxes in accordance with RA No. 7160.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the:
Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to pursue the immediate refund of the total amount of P44,750.86, otherwise, deduct it from the salary or from whatever money due the concerned Municipal officials/employees; and
Municipal Accountant to correct the account title used in recording the disallowed RATA from Other Receivables to Receivables-Disallowances/Charges account for proper and fair presentation of account balances in the financial statements (FS).

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MT to prepare and submit the IIRUP and undertake the necessary action for the disposal of the said properties in accordance with applicable rules and regulations.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the BAC Secretariat to consolidate the Municipality’s PPMPs and to stop the practice of procuring supplies and materials by piecemeal through shopping as an  alternative mode of procurement and adopt Public Bidding as the primary mode of procurement in compliance with RA No. 9184.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to reclassify the affected expense accounts in order to present fairly the FS at year-end and henceforth comply with the proper use of accounts in accordance with the Revised Chart of Accounts for Local Government Units.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the concerned Local Poverty Reduction Action Team (LPRAT) composed of the following members to spearhead the implementation of the above listed projects:
Chairperson: Local Chief Executive (LCE)
Co-Chairperson: A Civil Society Organization (CSO)- representative elected by the CSO Assembly
Members: Representatives from the Government, to wit:
Sangguniang Panlungsod/Bayan Member who is the Chairperson of the Committee on Appropriation;
Liga ng Barangay President;
All local government department heads such as the Planning Officer, Budget Officer, Fishery and Agriculture Officer, Social Welfare and Development Officer, Health Officer, Public Employment Service Office (PESO) Manager and Tourism Officer.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator (MPDC) to see to it that only projects that truly contribute to the development of the Municipality are included and charged to the 20% DF.
We further recommended the refund of the amount of P1,761,822.00.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the MBO to appropriate at least 1% of their IRA for LCPC and provide programs, projects and activities for the promotion of the rights and welfare of children as required under DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120.

We recommended the full utilization of the LDRRMF through the implementation of projects that will address disaster prevention and mitigation to strengthen and develop the LGU’s preparedness to natural and man-made hazards and disasters in accordance with the above cited law.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to see to it that the SCBAA or the Notes to FS includes disclosures pertaining to the budget and actual amounts, and the reconciliation of actual amounts on a comparable basis to the budget and actual amounts in the FS, in compliance with Paragraphs 14, 29 and 47 of PPSAS 24, to achieve transparency in financial reporting.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor require the:

Inventory Committee to conduct physical inventory of PPEs and prepare and submit the inventory report within the prescribed period; and


Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to prepare and maintain complete and updated PPE Ledger Cards and property cards, respectively, and require them to reconcile these records regularly.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor in coordination with the Sanguniang Bayan, adopt and implement cost measures to observe economy in the telephone and internet expenses:

Consider the use of post-paid plan offered by telecommunications network from P500.00 to P1,000.00 for head of offices and P2,000.00 to P2,500.00 per month for Municipal Mayor and Vice Mayor.

Review the amounts of E-Load allowance in relation to the available mobile phones and internet connection and consider its reduction.

Utilize alternatives modes of communication such as internet for long-distance communication.


Consider the features of communication networks of free long distance calls for Smart to Smart or Globe to Globe phones.

Set reasonable limits on use of mobile phones to the entitled officials. Any excess on the limit set shall be for the personal account of the users.

We also recommended that if cell cards/E-Load shall be allowed, consider purchase in bulk by the GSO, following the required procurement procedures and distribute the same in accordance with the guidelines.  

Further, we recommended to refund the total amount of:

P145,769.57 representing the amount paid for the Globe post-paid plan  as  second mobile phone provided to the Municipal Mayor;

P88,500.00 pertaining to the three months reimbursements for October to December, 2014 charged to current appropriation.

We also recommended to require the Municipal Accountant to submit the following documents:

Official Receipts for the purchases of E-Load.

Acknowledgement Receipt by the Department Heads for the receipt from the Vice-Mayor of E-Load allowances.

Canvass of prices for the purchases of E-Load.


Proof or evidence that expenses were incurred for official functions.

We recommended the Municipal Mayor to:

adopt and implement control measures to ensure that the amount granted for cellphone allowance is reasonable and used  for official business only; and

consider requiring the concerned officials to apply for a minimum mobile plan instead of granting cash for prepaid cellular cards and to fix the maximum amount that each official could charge/reimburse to avoid unnecessary/excessive expenses.
